title: Oatmeal raisin cookies
subtitle: Thick, chewy American-style oat cookies with raisins
excerpt: Thick, chewy American-style cookies made with rolled oats and raisins. The oats provide texture and chew; the light brown sugar adds a caramel note. Bake until the edges are set and the centre is still soft — they firm as they cool. Makes eighteen.
sourceNotes: Oatmeal cookies are documented in American domestic cookbooks from the late nineteenth century, many available through the Internet Archive. Fannie Merritt Farmer, The Boston Cooking-School Cook Book (1896), available through the Internet Archive: oatmeal cookies made with rolled oats, sugar, butter, and egg are documented as a standard American domestic biscuit. Raisins as a common addition are noted in American household baking manuals from the same period.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze baked cookies in a single layer, then bag. Defrost at room temperature.
recipe.batchNotes: Double the recipe and bake in two batches.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Keep in an airtight tin for 5 days. The dough can be refrigerated overnight before baking.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven, middle shelf. The cookies will look underdone when you pull them — the edges should be set but the centre soft. They firm as they cool on the tray

glossaryTerms.definition: A cookie made by dropping portions of soft dough onto a baking tray rather than rolling and cutting. The spread of a drop cookie is controlled by the fat content and temperature of the dough — warm dough spreads more, cold dough spreads less. For thick, chewy cookies, chill the dough for 20 minutes before baking; for thinner, crisper cookies, bake at room temperature. Each portion should be uniform in size so the cookies bake at the same rate.

content.text: Oatmeal cookies are done when the edges are set and the centre still looks raw. That is the point to pull them. They will firm on the tray as they cool to a dense, chewy texture. Leave them too long and they dry out; pull them too early and they collapse.

Preheat the oven to 175°C fan. Line two baking trays with baking paper. Whisk together the {{rolled-oats}}, {{plain-flour}}, {{bicarbonate-of-soda}}, {{mixed-spice}}, and {{sea-salt-fine}}. In a separate bowl, beat the {{unsalted-butter}} with the {{light-brown-sugar}} and {{caster-sugar}} until pale. Beat in the {{eggs}} and {{vanilla-extract}}. Fold in the dry ingredients, then the {{raisins}}. This is a

dough: drop heaped tablespoons onto the lined trays, spacing them 5 cm apart. Bake for 12–14 minutes until the edges are set and lightly coloured; the centres will look underdone. Cool on the tray for 10 minutes before moving to a rack.
